preamble: The shift from the full vowel [ʌ] (the 'strut' vowel) to the mid-central vowel [ə] (schwa) is a classic example of vowel reduction. In stress-timed languages like English, vowels in unstressed syllables lose their peripheral qualities, duration, and distinctiveness, centralizing toward the neutral schwa.

examples.note: When the prefix loses stress, the vowel reduces to schwa.
In Akanje, an underlying /o/ or /a/ reduces to [ʌ] immediately before the stress, but reduces further to [ə] in other unstressed positions.
